definition: Split binary codes into substrings and index each substring so Hamming-radius or top-k candidates can be found without scanning every code. Final Hamming distance is exact for emitted candidates.

scale_profile.best_for: Large collections of binary embeddings, hash codes, or bit-vector scientific fingerprints.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Millions to billions of binary codes when code length and radius are suitable.
scale_profile.avoid_when: The scientific similarity is Jaccard/Tanimoto rather than Hamming; use bitset or MinHash strategies instead.

tunable_parameters.name: hamming_radius_or_k
tunable_parameters.purpose: Defines retained neighbors or thresholded graph edges.

tunable_parameters.name: bit_weights
tunable_parameters.purpose: Optional weights for weighted Hamming distance.

quality_gates: Report bit length, code generation method, and whether Hamming or weighted Hamming was used.
Check exact Hamming distances after candidate retrieval.
Verify that binary compression preserves the scientific similarity of interest.
failure_modes: Hamming distance on learned hash codes may not reflect scientific novelty.
Large Hamming radii can still produce many candidates.
Weighted Hamming indexes require careful handling of bit weights.
example_usage: python binary_hamming_topk.py --train train.csv --eval eval.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --code-col binary_code --top-k 50

citations.title: Fast Exact Search in Hamming Space with Multi-Index Hashing
citations.evidence: Develops multi-index hashing for exact nearest-neighbor search in Hamming space with speedups over linear scans.

citations.title: Efficient Querying from Weighted Binary Codes
citations.evidence: Extends multi-index ideas to weighted binary codes for efficient nearest-neighbor querying.
